pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly injects intentional security flaws into the Oracle environment
setup_task.sh uses DBMS_RLS.ADD_POLICY, CREATE CONTEXT, SYS_CONTEXT, DBMS_SESSION.SET_CONTEXT, and the task requires creating Security Audit infrastructure

metadata_value.broken_policy_function: Returns NULL predicate instead of tenant_id filter
metadata_value.missing_policy: FINANCIAL_RECORDS table has no VPD policy

verified_value.broken_policy_function: Returns NULL predicate instead of tenant_id filter
verified_value.missing_policy: FINANCIAL_RECORDS table has no VPD policy

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 388, 370, 396 explicitly document and implement these three intentional bugs

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated multi-tenant SaaS data in an Oracle database. It has been explicitly verified that the setup script injects three intentional security flaws: (1) the TENANT_ISOLATION_POLICY function incorrectly returns a NULL predicate instead of filtering by tenant_id, (2) the FINANCIAL_RECORDS table is left completely unprotected without a VPD policy, and (3) the TENANT_CTX_PKG application context package incorrectly defaults to tenant_id 0 (superuser) instead of denying access. The task uses Oracle features such as DBMS_RLS (VPD), Application Contexts, Policy Functions, and DBMS_SESSION, and requires the creation of security audit infrastructure. The expected output is evaluated via /tmp/multi_tenant_result.json.
